name: Armenia

outbound_official.notes: Armenia's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (mfa.am) does not publish a completed outbound visa-free list for Armenian ordinary passport holders. The MFA visa page (mfa.am/en/visa/) contains a section titled 'Visa requirements for Armenian citizens' with a country/territory dropdown, but as of June 2026 this section is explicitly noted as 'in development phase' - no data is published there. The bilateral visa-free regime page (mfa.am/en/whoneedvisa) lists countries with which Armenia has mutual agreements, but is framed from the inbound (entry-to-Armenia) perspective, not as an authoritative outbound list for Armenian citizens. No separate official portal, e-gov page, or consular publication listing countries Armenian ordinary passport holders may enter without a prior consular visa was located. Rely on destination-side data and the bilateral agreements at mfa.am/en/whoneedvisa as the closest proxy.

e_visa.nationality: Countries not eligible for visa-free entry and not on the invitation-only list
e_visa.notes: Armenian e-visa available via evisa.mfa.am. Visitor visa up to 21 days: non-refundable fee AMD 3,000 (approx. USD 8). Visitor visa up to 120 days: non-refundable fee AMD 15,000 (approx. USD 38–39). Multiple-entry e-visas also available (60 days / 6-month validity: USD 52; 120 days / 1-year validity: USD 103). E-visa portal is operated by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Individual nationality eligibility must be checked via the portal at evisa.mfa.am/check-eligibility. Certain nationalities require an invitation and can only apply at Armenian diplomatic missions - see https://www.mfa.am/en/invitation_only

visa_policy.notes: Unilateral visa-free list last updated 02.02.2024 per official MFA page. Bilateral agreements list also updated 02.02.2024. Armenian visas issued for a maximum of 120 days with possible 60-day extension. No visa on arrival as a default border policy; ordinary e-visas and mission visas must generally be obtained in advance. Citizens staying visa-free may stay up to 180 days per year. Diplomatic/service passport bilateral agreements for select countries are captured in conditional_access. The temporary 113-country credential-based exemption (Jan 1 – Jul 1, 2026) is captured in conditional_access and has now expired as of July 1, 2026; no official extension was found as of this validation (June 29, 2026).

credential.issuer: US | EU Member States | Schengen Area States | UAE | Bahrain | Qatar | Saudi Arabia | Kuwait | Oman
credential.subtype: physical residence permit card or passport sticker; must display document title, given name, surname, nationality, date of birth, and validity period in Latin script with Gregorian calendar dates

conditional_access.conditions: EXPIRED July 1, 2026. Temporary visa exemption ran from January 1 to July 1, 2026. No official extension announced as of June 29, 2026. Residence permit must have been valid for at least 6 months from date of entry into Armenia. Permit must be presented as a physical card or passport sticker - mobile IDs, electronic cards, scanned copies, and photographs are NOT accepted. Permit must display in Latin script: the document title, given name, surname, nationality, date of birth, and validity period in Gregorian calendar format (e.g. 18.06.2027 or 18 JUN 2027). Up to 180 days within a one-year period. UK residence permits are NOT confirmed as qualifying under official MFA sources; only US, EU/Schengen, and GCC (UAE, Bahrain, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Oman) are confirmed. Full 113-country list dated 30.12.2025 at https://www.mfa.am/filemanager/consular/visa/2025/2025-12-30-LIST-OF-COUNTRIES-TEMPORARY-VISA-EXEMPTION.pdf.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa (up to 21 days, single entry)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term visit to Armenia for tourism, personal, or business purposes. Covers stays not exceeding 21 days.
visa_types.notes: AMD 3,000 / USD 8 / EUR 7. Available via e-visa system (evisa.mfa.am, 3 working days processing) or in-person at Armenian diplomatic missions (10 working days). Fee is non-refundable. Armenia does not issue visas on arrival at border checkpoints.

visa_types.name: Visitor Visa (up to 120 days, single entry)
visa_types.purpose: Extended short-term visit to Armenia for tourism, personal, or business purposes. Covers stays up to 120 days.
visa_types.notes: AMD 15,000 / USD 39 / EUR 34. Available via e-visa system (evisa.mfa.am, 3 working days processing) or in-person at Armenian diplomatic missions (10 working days). Fee is non-refundable. Visa may be extended by up to 60 days inside Armenia.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visitor Visa (60 days stay / 6-month validity)
visa_types.purpose: Multiple-entry visitor visa for frequent travelers to Armenia for tourism or business. Allows cumulative stays of up to 60 days within a 6-month validity window.
visa_types.notes: AMD 20,000 / USD 52 / EUR 46. Available via e-visa system (evisa.mfa.am) or diplomatic missions. Fee is non-refundable. Armenia uses a unified 'visitor visa' category for both tourism and business purposes.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visitor Visa (120 days stay / 1-year validity)
visa_types.purpose: Long-term multiple-entry visitor visa for frequent travelers to Armenia for tourism or business. Allows cumulative stays of up to 120 days within a 1-year validity window.
visa_types.notes: AMD 40,000 / USD 103 / EUR 91. Available via e-visa system (evisa.mfa.am) or diplomatic missions. Fee is non-refundable. Armenia uses a unified 'visitor visa' category for both tourism and business purposes. No separate work, student, or digital nomad visa categories exist in the official fee schedule.

visa_types.name: Single Entry Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows a single transit through Armenian territory en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: AMD 10,000 / USD 26 / EUR 23. Available via e-visa system (evisa.mfa.am) or diplomatic missions. Fee is non-refundable.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Transit Visa (1-year validity)
visa_types.purpose: Allows multiple transits through Armenian territory over a 1-year period, for frequent travelers passing through Armenia.
visa_types.notes: AMD 18,000 / USD 47 / EUR 41. Available via e-visa system (evisa.mfa.am) or diplomatic missions. Fee is non-refundable.

visa_types.name: Official Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued free of charge to holders of official/service passports traveling on official government business.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Issued at Armenian diplomatic missions. Many countries have bilateral agreements for visa-free access for official/service passport holders - see conditional_access.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued free of charge to holders of diplomatic passports traveling on diplomatic mission.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge. Issued at Armenian diplomatic missions. Many countries have bilateral agreements for visa-free access for diplomatic passport holders - see conditional_access.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists in Armenia per official government sources (mfa.am, gov.am). Citizenship is obtained through: ethnic Armenian descent, 3-year permanent lawful residence, marriage to an Armenian citizen (2 years registered marriage + 365 days lawful residence in Armenia), having an Armenian parent/child, restoration of previously renounced citizenship, or refugee/stateless person status. Per the official MFA citizenship page, obtaining another citizenship or unilaterally renouncing Armenian citizenship does NOT automatically result in loss of Armenian citizenship - dual citizenship is therefore effectively permitted. Processing time for citizenship application: 90 workdays. State fee for acquisition: 50,000 AMD.

programs.name: Temporary Resident Card
programs.notes: Issued for up to 1 year, renewable annually. Eligible if: studying in Armenia; holding work permit in Armenia; spouse/parent/child of a Temporary Resident Card holder; relative of Armenian citizen or Special Passport holder; running business activity in Armenia; Armenian national; or other cases prescribed by law. Fee: 105,000 AMD (US citizens: 85,000 AMD). Processing time: up to 30 days. 3 years of lawful permanent residence enables citizenship application.

programs.name: Permanent Resident Card
programs.notes: Issued for 5 years, extendable by 5 years. Eligible if: running business activity in Armenia; relative of Armenian citizen or Special Passport holder who has legally resided in Armenia for 3 years and has adequate accommodation/property; Armenian national. Fee: 140,000 AMD (US citizens: 90,000 AMD). Processing time: up to 30 days. No specific minimum investment amount stated on official page.

programs.name: Special Residency Status (Special Passport)
programs.notes: Granted by the Prime Minister for 10 years. Available to foreign citizens of Armenian ancestry and distinguished individuals who have provided significant services to the Armenian state and nation or are engaged in economic/cultural activities in Armenia. Holders receive a Special Armenian Passport, no visa required for Armenia, and enjoy rights/obligations of Armenian citizens except voting, running for office, and military service. State duty: 150,000 AMD. Processing time: approximately 3-4 months. Not an investment-specific program - no minimum investment stated. Genocide survivors receive this through a facilitated fee-waived procedure.

agreements: Eurasian Economic Union (EAEU) - free movement of labor; Armenia is a member along with Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, and Russian Federation

validation_notes: 2026-06-13 re-validation: Re-fetched key official Armenian MFA embassy pages (dubai.mfa.am, kuwait.mfa.am, canada.mfa.am, india.mfa.am). Main mfa.am domain was blocked by Radware DDoS protection during this validation pass.
CORRECTED: e-visa note updated to add multiple-entry e-visa options (60-day/6-month at USD 52; 120-day/1-year at USD 103) confirmed via Armenia Embassy to Canada fees page (effective Jan 1, 2026).

CORRECTED: conditional_access credential entry - clarified that UK residence permits are NOT confirmed as qualifying issuers per official MFA embassy announcements (dubai.mfa.am, kuwait.mfa.am), which consistently name only US, EU/Schengen, and GCC (UAE, Bahrain, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, Oman). Removed the claim that UK qualifies; added explicit note in conditions field.

CORRECTED: conditional_access credential entry - added explicit note that mobile IDs, electronic cards, scanned copies, and photographs are NOT accepted as qualifying credential formats, per official Kuwait embassy clarification.

CORRECTED: conditional_access credential entry - source_url updated from generic mfa.am/en/visa/ to the specific Kuwait embassy official clarification page which contains the most complete official terms.

CORRECTED: Montenegro conditional_access entry - removed erroneous seasonal ordinary-passport note (April–October exemption). Montenegro ordinary passports are on the unilateral exemption list (visa_free) year-round at 180 days/year; the seasonal reference was an artifact of older bilateral data.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: The visa_free unilateral list (mfa.am/en/visafreelist) and bilateral list (mfa.am/en/whoneedvisa) match the prior record - mfa.am main domain blocked by Radware DDoS protection during this pass; no changes confirmed.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: cbi.has_program=false - no CBI program confirmed on any official source.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: rbi programs - fees and structures match the prior record.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: e-visa fee schedule confirmed via canada.mfa.am/en/fees/ - rates as of Jan 1, 2026 match the prior record.
2026-06-27 re-validation: Re-fetched kuwait.mfa.am, dubai.mfa.am, canada.mfa.am, and the official 113-country PDF (via PyMuPDF). Main mfa.am domain remained blocked by Radware DDoS protection.

CORRECTED: Added 'Democratic People's Republic of Korea' (DPRK/North Korea) to conditional_access eligible_nationalities - entry #27 in the official 113-country PDF (30.12.2025). This nationality was previously omitted from the conditional_access list, an oversight now corrected.
CORRECTED: Normalised 'Cote d'Ivoire' to 'Côte d'Ivoire' in conditional_access eligible_nationalities to match the exact official PDF spelling.
CONFIRMED: The 113-country PDF country count cross-checked - exactly 113 entries confirmed. All other nationalities in conditional_access match the official PDF.

2026-06-29 re-validation: Re-fetched kuwait.mfa.am, dubai.mfa.am, canada.mfa.am. Main mfa.am domain remains blocked by Radware DDoS protection. Visa fee schedule at canada.mfa.am/en/fees/ confirmed unchanged (AMD/USD rates as of Jan 1, 2026).

CORRECTED: conditional_access credential entry conditions field updated to note that this exemption EXPIRED July 1, 2026. No official extension announcement found as of June 29, 2026 from any official mfa.am domain source.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: visa_policy.visa_free list - mfa.am main domain still blocked; could not re-fetch visafreelist or whoneedvisa pages directly. Lists preserved per preservation rule (cannot confirm or deny changes without official source access). No changes made to visa_free or bilateral lists.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: e-visa fees confirmed again via canada.mfa.am/en/fees/ at same rates.

NOT RECORDED: Aggregator sources (visahq.com, travelandtourworld.com) claim a 'GCC visa-free bloc agreement signed February 22, 2026' granting UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Qatar nationals 90-day ordinary-passport visa-free access. This was NOT confirmed on any official mfa.am source. UAE, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia are already in the visa_free unilateral list at 180 days; the 90-day claim contradicts this and the sources are non-official aggregators. Not recorded per safety rules.
gaps: The full enumerated list of 113 countries covered by the temporary visa exemption is in a PDF at https://www.mfa.am/filemanager/consular/visa/2025/2025-12-30-LIST-OF-COUNTRIES-TEMPORARY-VISA-EXEMPTION.pdf - all 113 countries have been enumerated in conditional_access from the official PDF (30.12.2025) and confirmed via official MFA embassy sources.
The invitation-only country list (mfa.am/en/invitation_only) - nationalities required to apply only at Armenian diplomatic missions - was not individually enumerated; only the URL is referenced.

E-visa eligibility by nationality is not individually listed; the official portal evisa.mfa.am is a JavaScript SPA requiring interactive nationality selection. The e-visa portal SSL certificate was expired during a prior validation pass (June 2026).

Visa on arrival (VOA) at Zvartnots Airport: The official Kuwait embassy clarification states that citizens of certain countries (named examples: Egypt, Iraq, Philippines, India) can obtain an entry visa on arrival if their residence card is valid for less than 6 months but at least 1 day. This VOA-on-credential pathway is NOT captured in visa_on_arrival (which remains empty) because the precise nationality list and full terms were not confirmed on a primary official MFA page. This should be investigated further when mfa.am is accessible.

Turkey (Türkiye): From January 1, 2026, Armenian and Turkish diplomatic, special, and service passport holders may obtain an e-visa free of charge (fee waived, not unconditionally visa-free). Ordinary Turkish passport holders require a standard visa. Source: https://www.mfa.am/en/press-releases/2025/12/29/Armenia_Turkiye/13710

UK residence permits: Multiple aggregator sources claim UK residence permits qualify for the 113-country exemption, but official MFA embassy communications (dubai.mfa.am, kuwait.mfa.am) do not mention the UK in the qualifying issuer list. Cannot confirm or deny without direct access to mfa.am/en/visa/.

The 113-country temporary credential-based exemption expired July 1, 2026. As of June 29, 2026 (this validation), no official extension has been announced. The conditional_access entry is retained with EXPIRED notation. If an extension is announced, this record must be updated promptly.
No official golden visa or investor-residence program with specific investment thresholds found on Armenian government sources.
No official digital-nomad, startup, or fast-track skilled-worker scheme found on official sources.
CIS free movement treaty specifics were not individually verified via an official CIS portal.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) or similar diaspora/special-status conditional entry rule found on any official Armenian government source.

Aggregator sources claim a 'GCC visa-free bloc agreement signed February 22, 2026 granting UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Qatar nationals 90-day visa-free access.' UAE, Qatar and Saudi Arabia are already unconditionally visa-free under the confirmed unilateral exemption at 180 days/year. The 90-day claim from aggregators contradicts the confirmed 180-day unilateral exemption; no official mfa.am source confirms such a new agreement. Not separately recorded; needs official confirmation.

Main mfa.am domain has been blocked by Radware DDoS protection on every validation pass since June 2026. The visa_free unilateral list (mfa.am/en/visafreelist) and bilateral list (mfa.am/en/whoneedvisa) cannot be directly re-verified until the domain becomes accessible again.
